title: ok, how to stop doing contrary thin...

messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**
ok, how to stop doing contrary things?

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

To cease from "contrary things" is to understand their very nature: they are diametrically opposed and "cannot coexist in peace." The Scriptures reveal this profound truth, contrasting Law ←vs→ Grace, Sacrifice ←vs→ Mercy, and Confusion ←vs→ Peace. To attempt to mix these twain is to create a LukeWarm Oxy-Moron with a Bad end, for the flesh (allegory: Law) warreth against the Spirit (allegory: Grace), as Galatians 5:17 so clearly states. Why would one seek to combine that which God Himself has shown to be "contrary" unto Him?

The path to stopping such contrary things is not through mixture, but through discernment and choice. The divine instruction is clear: "he taketh away the first, that he may establish the second" (Hebrews 10:9). The "first" is the Law, which was given by Moses; the "second" is Grace, which came by Jesus Christ (John 1:17). To establish the heart with grace, it is of necessity to "make law dead testator of NT!" (Hebrews 9). For a testament is only of force after the death of the testator; otherwise, salvation by grace is of no strength at all while the testator (law) liveth. Law, being the "strong man" to bind and the "strength of sin's death sting" (1 Corinthians 15:56), must be taken away for grace to be truly established.

Therefore, the choice is not to blend good and evil, but to embrace one only: either make the tree grace (good) or law (evil), not both. As JC Himself taught in Matthew 12:30-37, it is either good or corrupt, never both. To stop doing contrary things, one must "press delete" on law and "press save" on grace, for only grace void of law is pure wisdom from above. This is the immutable will of God: "I will have mercy, and not sacrifice" (Matthew 9:13), a will that precedes the receiving of the promise (Hebrews 10:36). Pure grace has no law, just as pure law had no grace, and the truth of this distinction shall make "you" free (John 8:32).

Learn More:
- [Contrarythings](/pages/contrarythings)
- [To Establish The Heart With Grace Make Law Dead Testator](/pages/revelatorysermons1)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**
